title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: This blog post provides detailed coverage from the 2026 International Communication Association (ICA) conference in Cape Town, summarizing Fabio Giglietto's presentation and research on partisan alignment, quality journalism, and algorithmic amplification on Facebook.
description: An academic blog post summarizing Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the ICA 2026 conference regarding the impact of Facebook's algorithmic tweaks on news URL engagement.

title: M/C Journal 'twitter' Issue Now Out!
snippet: Media coverage and announcement detailing the release of the special 'twitter' issue of M/C Journal co-edited for Twitter's 20th anniversary. Professor Fabio Giglietto is highlighted as one of the prominent social media researchers contributing reflections on Twitter's legacy and the future of social media.
description: An announcement on snurb.info regarding the release of the special 'twitter' issue of M/C Journal, highlighting Professor Fabio Giglietto's contributions as a prominent social media researcher reflecting on the platform's legacy.

title: socialmediaandsociety.org
description: An announcement for the International Conference on Social Media & Society listing Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist discussing future pathways for social media research following the closure of CrowdTangle.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino
description: News article from Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: Press coverage from Vivere Urbino discussing the societal and political ripples of Fabio Giglietto's study, 'A Pretty Blunt Approach.' The article notes how Giglietto's findings on algorithmic visibility were featured on a Rai3 'Report' TV investigation and prompted formal inquiries in the European Parliament.
description: An article in Vivere Urbino reporting on the societal and political impact of Fabio Giglietto's study 'A Pretty Blunt Approach' regarding Meta's algorithms, highlighting its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: L'articolo 21 nella gabbia di Facebook
snippet: A journalistic analysis exploring internet-era challenges to freedom of speech (Article 21 of the Italian Constitution). It highlights the findings of Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts to assess the drop in visibility for Italian parliamentarians.
description: A journalistic analysis discussing Fabio Giglietto's computational social science research on Facebook algorithmic visibility drops for Italian parliamentarians and its implications for digital freedom of speech.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: A news feature covering the research findings published by Fabio Giglietto. It details his team's analysis of over 2.5 million Facebook posts and how Meta's algorithmic suppression mechanisms are failing against bad actors while suppressing democratic communication from elected representatives.
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE
description: News article from Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article reports on a major study led by Fabio Giglietto analyzing Meta's algorithms [2.2.1]. The study reveals that Meta's policies to reduce political content disproportionately limited the organic visibility of institutional, official politicians while boosting the relative reach of extremist and conspiracy-oriented accounts.
description: A news article from Open.online reporting on a major study led by Fabio Giglietto at the University of Urbino, which details how Meta's algorithms penalize official political accounts while boosting extremist content.
